summary: Completed testing of WhatsApp broadcast feature with background job processing, progress bars, and separate message lists. All 23 backend tests passed. Frontend UI verified with progress bar polling working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/admin/whatsapp/send-welcome/{event_id} returns job_id immediately (no timeout)
Backend: GET /api/admin/whatsapp/job/{job_id} returns real-time progress (status, total, sent, failed, processed)
Backend: GET /api/admin/whatsapp/status/{event_id} returns separate 'welcome' and 'assignment' sections
Backend: POST /api/admin/whatsapp/retry-failed/{event_id} returns job_id for polling
Backend: All endpoints require authentication (401/403 without token)
Backend: Job polling shows increasing processed count over time
Backend: Send welcome/assignments returns in <5 seconds (no HTTP timeout)
Frontend: WhatsApp tab shows separate Welcome Messages and Table Assignment Messages sections
Frontend: Send Welcome button triggers background job and shows progress bar
Frontend: Progress bar shows Processing count, percentage, Sent, Skipped, Failed
Frontend: Progress bar auto-updates via polling every 1.5 seconds
Frontend: Toast notification shows 'Broadcasting to X users (Y already sent)'
Frontend: Send button changes to 'Sending...' with spinner during processing
Frontend: Delivery Log shows 768 messages with user names and phone numbers
Frontend: Retry Failed button present and functional
Frontend: Refresh button present and functional
Frontend: Send Assignments button present (disabled when no table assignments)
critical_code_review_comments: In-memory _jobs dict will lose job state on server restart - acceptable for MVP but consider Redis for production
WhatsApp API (flexiwaba) not configured in test env - all messages fail as expected

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all UI elements verified, progress bar working)

seed_data_creation: None - used existing test event 06e81655-7341-4435-a3c3-5e2b9f691f18 with 496 registrations
context_for_next_testing_agent: WhatsApp broadcast feature fully implemented with background job processing. Key endpoints: POST /send-welcome/{event_id}, POST /send-assignments/{event_id}, GET /job/{job_id} for polling, GET /status/{event_id} returns welcome/assignment sub-objects. Frontend uses startPolling() with 1.5s interval. Progress component from @/components/ui/progress. WhatsApp API (flexiwaba) not configured in test env so all messages fail - this is expected behavior. In-memory _jobs dict tracks job progress.
